page: Microsoft Azure F48als_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Falsv7 family, running under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The system features 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The processor includes an L1 data cache of 48 KB, an L2 cache of 1.0 MB, and an L3 cache of 32 MB. It supports advanced vector extensions including AVX, AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AVX_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, alongside cryptographic acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.

In benchmark evaluations, the F48als_v7 demonstrates top-tier performance, placing in the top 10% for both single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests. Its memory subsystem achieves top-tier results in small-block read-only memory bandwidth and large-block write memory bandwidth, while large-block read-only bandwidth remains strong in the top 25%. Computational efficiency is reflected in top-tier scores for single-threaded gzip compression and both single- and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. The instance also registers top-tier performance in PassMark CPU, extended instructions, and physics tests, alongside strong encryption capabilities.

The instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, though it is premium storage capable. This lack of local storage requires reliance on external network-attached storage. The 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU represents a low-memory configuration, making it less suitable for memory-intensive databases or large-scale in-memory processing. However, it performs exceptionally well in database operations and Redis SET operations, scoring in the top 10%. For machine learning workloads, the server achieves top-tier and strong performance in prompt processing and text generation for small and medium LLM models, though performance drops to average for large 70B models. This profile makes the instance highly efficient for compute-bound workloads, web serving, and medium-scale database operations where raw CPU speed is prioritized over memory capacity.
description: Microsoft Azure F48als_v7 is a compute-optimized server instance featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 physical cores operating at 4.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Configured with 96.0 GB of RAM, it provides a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. The instance lacks bundled local storage and GPUs but is premium storage capable. Benchmark data places the F48als_v7 in the top 10% for single-core and multi-core CPU performance, as well as database operations and Redis SET throughput. It also exhibits strong memory bandwidth and high-efficiency compression capabilities. Due to its high clock speed, dedicated core allocation, and advanced vector extensions like AVX-512, this instance is suited for compute-intensive tasks, web serving, and database operations that do not require massive memory footprints.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F48als_v7 is a compute-optimized server with 48 AMD EPYC cores at 4.5 GHz and 96 GB RAM. It delivers top-tier CPU and database performance but lacks local storage and GPU resources.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F48als_v7 is a compute-optimized server featuring 48 AMD EPYC cores, 96 GB RAM, and top-tier CPU benchmark performance.
tagline: A compute-optimized server featuring forty-eight high-frequency AMD EPYC cores and a low memory-to-core ratio for intensive processing workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 cores running at 4.5 GHz
96.0 GB RAM providing a 2.0 GB per core low-memory ratio
Top-tier single-core and multi-core CPU benchmark performance
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Suited for compute-heavy workloads, web serving, and database operations
